relations.note: The masthead gradient rule specifies a dedicated layout alignment and contextual pairing for one of the pre-approved gradients.

quotes: ### Gradient Specifications (approved set — never invent gradients)

rules.intent: Maintain brand visual consistency by restricting gradients to pre-approved color combinations.
rules.rule_text: Only the approved gradient specifications may be used; do not invent new gradients. Approved sets are: Brand Blue to Dark Navy (#00529b to #011E45), Sky Blue to Deep Blue (#358CCB to #003D74), Sunshine to 75% Gold (#faa31b to Gold @75%), Golden Sand to White to Light Blue (#FEF1C8 to #ffffff to #E6F0F9), Golden Sand to 75% Gold to Sunshine (#FEF1C8 to Gold @75% to #faa31b), and Sunshine to Light Gold (#faa31b to #ffe05c).

quotes: Sunshine → 0C,10M,78Y,0K (≈`#ffe05c` light gold) | `#faa31b` → light gold | **Masthead gradient — the gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast; mark sits at the lightest end**
Gradients run horizontally in masthead/banner applications (dark/saturated → light, left → right as shown).

rules.intent: Ensure legibility and approved contrast ratio for the logo mark against the masthead gradient.
rules.rule_text: The Sunshine to Light Gold gradient (color.primary.sunshine = #faa31b to color.primary.light_gold = #ffe05c) must be used as the masthead gradient with the logo mark. Gradients in masthead and banner applications must run horizontally from dark/saturated on the left to light on the right, with the logo mark positioned at the lightest end for optimal contrast.
